summary_one_line: A structurally valid sfnt font carrying a GPOS table (table_index==1) and variation coords (font->num_coords>0) so the true branch fires.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: A well-formed sfnt header (00 01 00 00 sfnt version + table count) with a recognized 4-byte table tag such as 'GPOS' at the table-directory offset, so a GPOS table is parsed and the layout-apply context is constructed at table_index==1.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: cmplog winner seeds contain the literal tag bytes 'GPOS' (47 50 4f 53) and 'GPO' at offsets 0x0c-0x0f, exactly the tag-comparison operands that table-tag dispatch checks; offset 0x0c-0x0f W bytes are concentrated on G/P/O/S while L bytes are diffuse.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: naive loser seeds are short and malformed (6-64 bytes) with diffuse, non-tag bytes at 0x0c-0x0f, so no valid GPOS table tag is matched and layout-apply for table_index==1 is never entered.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: cmplog's I2SRandReplace reads intercepted tag-CMP operands from the CmpLogObserver buffer and substitutes the expected tag bytes (e.g. 'GPOS') into the input, planting the exact table tag that naive must hit by blind havoc.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: Same GPOS table-tag / sfnt structure requirement, where the input progressively approaches the required tag and header field values that gate the table_index==1 layout context.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: value_profile beats naive (resolved 9/10 vs 2/10) by preserving intermediate inputs whose CMP operands are closer to the required tag/header constants, building a gradient toward the structurally valid font.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: naive has no CMP-distance feedback, so partial-match progress toward the tag/header constants is not preserved as corpus and the structure is never assembled.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: value_profile's CMP_MAP records operand-distance buckets (Hamming / matching-prefix length) and a new closer-matching bucket is preserved as corpus, giving a gradient toward the tag and header constants naive cannot follow.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: An input that drives the layout machinery deep enough (matcher/skipping-iterator/ChainContext dispatch) that the constructor at table_index==1 with num_coords is reached on a call path distinct from shallow parsing.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: naive_ctx records (call-context, edge) pairs as new coverage; the same downstream functions (matcher_t setters, skipping_iterator_t, ChainContext::dispatch) reached via the GPOS-apply call path register as fresh coverage, rewarding the deeper exploration the blocker needs.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: naive sees only bare edges, so revisiting matcher/iterator code via a deeper call path yields no new coverage and the seeds that would reach the table_index==1 constructor are not retained.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: naive_ctx's CtxHook folds the caller chain into the edge-map index, so reaching the layout-apply functions through a new call context counts as new coverage and steers corpus toward the deeper GPOS-apply path.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: Same structural GPOS/variable-font target, reached by spending more havoc mutations on the small, stable, fast seeds that are closest to a valid font header.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: minimizer's calibrated power stage assigns more mutations to faster/smaller/more-stable seeds; the compact valid-header seeds (e.g. the 141-179 byte font-shaped winner seeds) earn extra energy and are mutated harder toward the structural target.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: naive uses a plain mutational stage with uniform energy, so it does not concentrate mutation budget on the promising small font-header seeds and stalls (resolved 2/10, 21.2h blocked).
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: minimizer's CalibrationStage + StdPowerMutationalStage derive per-seed energy from intrinsic calibration (speed/size/stability), spending disproportionate mutation budget on the compact valid-header seeds that approach the structural gate.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker is a conjunction gate requiring table_index==1 AND font->num_coords on the true branch.
The true (blocked) side is the globally blocked side at this branch.
cmplog resolves the branch where naive does not (10/10 vs 2/10), with delta technique I2S.
cmplog winner seeds carry the literal 'GPOS' table tag bytes.
At offsets 0x0c-0x0f the winner bytes concentrate on G/P/O/S tag characters while loser bytes are diffuse.
naive loser seeds are short and malformed (e.g. 6 bytes).
evidence_trail.exact_quote: Seed 1 (id=003817559785f774, size=6 bytes, fuzzer=naive, trial=1, discovered_at=0s, mutation_op=ByteDecMutator,WordAddMutator):

evidence_trail.claim: cmplog's I2SRandReplace substitutes intercepted CMP operands into the input at matching offsets.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
`I2SRandReplace` reads the post-execution `CmpLogObserver` buffer,
picks a CMP entry, scans the input for byte sequences matching one
operand, and substitutes the other operand at those offsets.

evidence_trail.claim: value_profile resolves the branch over naive with delta technique value_profile.
value_profile preserves an input whose CMP operands are closer to matching as new corpus.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
An input that produces a
CMP-operand pair closer to matching than any previously-seen pair
adds a new CMP_MAP bucket and is preserved as corpus.

evidence_trail.claim: naive_ctx resolves the branch over naive with delta technique ctx_coverage.
naive_ctx folds the call context into the edge-map index so the same edge counts at different slots by call path.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
The hook
keeps a running hash of the current call context (caller chain) and
folds it into the edge-map index, so the same basic-block edge is
recorded at different map slots depending on the call path that
reached it.

evidence_trail.claim: The downstream layout functions (matcher setters, skipping iterator, ChainContext dispatch) fire far more in W than L.
minimizer resolves the branch over naive with delta technique calibrated_energy.
minimizer's power stage gives more mutations to faster/smaller/more-stable seeds via calibration-based perf_score.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
faster, smaller,
more-stable seeds earn more mutations

evidence_trail.claim: minimizer winner seeds are compact and font-shaped (141-179 bytes with valid sfnt header).

mechanism_consistency_check.verification_method: Ran `python3 tools/db_query.py lineage --branch 5517 --role W --fuzzer cmplog --trial 1 --seed 002c9ad92acce91b`; the chain has 31 ancestor levels and the trailing I2S-floor signal reports 2 dash rows (mutation_op = -) at depth 29 and 30, direct lower-bound evidence of an I2SRandReplace find in this winner's cmplog ancestry. Note this verifies only the cmplog>naive (I2S) hypothesis; the other three pairs carry distinct mechanisms (CMP_MAP gradient, context-sensitive coverage, calibrated energy) per multi_feature.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness gating on the same conjunction (table_index==1 && num_coords) in which cmplog does NOT outperform naive when the GPOS table tag is the only blind-search barrier — i.e. removing the tag-CMP barrier closes the cmplog>naive gap, showing the divergence was not I2S tag-substitution.

weakest_evidence_point: Attributing the cmplog win specifically to GPOS-tag I2S substitution rests on the tag bytes appearing in winner seeds plus a 2-row dash floor; the dash rows confirm SOME I2S find but not that it planted the table tag rather than another header constant.
